headline: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) Stock Could Be 18% Expensive Following Macau Revenue Pressure
summary: Las Vegas Sands stock has fallen sharply year to date, yet its valuation signals do not line up neatly. The Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) intrinsic value estimate points to a premium, while earnings based multiples screen the shares as relatively inexpensive. Year to date, Las Vegas Sands is down 28.4%, which has reset expectations around what counts as a reasonable entry price. Investor focus is firmly on how Macau margin pressure and intense competition could affect future cash flows, while...
